title: The Ten Commandments

evidence.note: Published 1923; the 95-year US copyright term has expired for works published through 1930, so the film print is public domain in the US by term expiry.

watch.label: Internet Archive — The Ten Commandments (1923), full film

editorial: The Ten Commandments is a 1923 American silent religious epic produced and directed by Cecil B. DeMille for Famous Players-Lasky (Paramount), written by Jeanie Macpherson. Structured as a biblical prologue filmed partly in early two-color Technicolor, with a celebrated parting of the Red Sea, plus a modern morality tale, it was a major box-office success and the first of DeMille's biblical spectacles. Published in 1923, the film print's 95-year US copyright term has expired, placing the photoplay in the public domain by term expiry.
